sequences.name: Initial digit of n.
Sums of three squares: numbers of the form x^2 + y^2 + z^2.
Numbers of form x^2 + 2y^2 + 2yz + 4z^2.
Numbers of form x^2 + y^2 + 2*z^2.
n written in base where place values are positive cubes.
Label a 1-cm ruler with digits 1 cm wide.
The nonnegative integers.
Numbers with an odd number of digits.
The zero sequence.
Number of trees of diameter 5.
Crossing number of complete graph with n nodes.
Nearest integer to modified Bessel function K_n(5).
Number of n-node rooted trees of height 5.
Binomial coefficients C(n,5).
Number of n-node rooted trees of height 6.
Number of n-node rooted trees of height 7.
Number of n-node rooted trees of height 8.
Number of permutations of [n] in which the longest increasing run has length 6.
The simplest sequence of positive numbers: the all 1's sequence.
From von Staudt-Clausen representation of Bernoulli numbers: a(n) = Bernoulli(2n) + Sum_{(p-1)|2n} 1/p.
Number of partitions of n into 3 squares (allowing part zero).
Pentanacci numbers: a(n) = a(n-1) + a(n-2) + a(n-3) + a(n-4) + a(n-5) with a(0) = a(1) = a(2) = a(3) = a(4) = 1.
Expansion of f(-q^3) * f(-q^8) * chi(-q^12) / chi(-q) in powers of q where chi(), f() are Ramanujan theta functions.
Hexanacci numbers with a(0) = ... = a(5) = 1.
"First factor" (or relative class number) h- for cyclotomic field Q( exp(2 Pi / prime(n)) ).
Triangle giving number L(n,k) of normalized k X n Latin rectangles.
Number of ways of making change for n cents using coins of 1, 5, 10, 25 cents.
Number of ways of making change for n cents using coins of 1, 5, 10, 25, 50 cents.
Number of classes of primitive positive definite binary quadratic forms of discriminant D = -4n; or equivalently the class number of the quadratic order of discriminant D = -4n.
Number of primitive n-bead necklaces (turning over is allowed) where complements are equivalent.
Number of n-bead necklaces with beads of 2 colors and primitive period n, when turning over is not allowed but the two colors can be interchanged.
Number of trees with n unlabeled nodes.
Number of partitions of n into 5 squares.
Number of partitions of n into 6 squares.
Number of n-node unrooted quartic trees; number of n-carbon alkanes C(n)H(2n+2) ignoring stereoisomers.
Number of n-node unrooted steric quartic trees; number of n-carbon alkanes C(n)H(2n+2) taking stereoisomers into account.
Number of 3-valent trees (= boron trees or binary trees) with n nodes.
Erroneous version of A040082.
Smallest prime power >= n.
Mosaic numbers or multiplicative projection of n: if n = Product (p_j^k_j) then a(n) = Product (p_j * k_j).
The positive integers. Also called the natural numbers, the whole numbers or the counting numbers, but these terms are ambiguous.
Euler's "numerus idoneus" (or "numeri idonei", or idoneal, or suitable, or convenient numbers).
Powers of primes. Alternatively, 1 and the prime powers (p^k, p prime, k >= 1).
Related to S(n), the number of self-dual monotone Boolean functions of n variables (A001206): 2^n-th term is S(n).
Union of all numbers {p, q} where p and q are both primes or powers of primes and q = p+2.
Numbers k such that k / (sum of digits of k) is a square.
Numbers k such that (k / product of digits of k) is 1 or a prime.
Numbers n such that n / product of digits of n is a square.
Dying rabbits: a(0) = 1; for 1 <= n <= 12, a(n) = Fibonacci(n); for n >= 13, a(n) = a(n-1) + a(n-2) - a(n-13).
Number of monosubstituted alkanes C(n-1)H(2n-1)-X with n-1 carbon atoms that are not stereoisomers.
Number of non-stereoisomeric paraffins with n carbon atoms.
Number of partitions of n into relatively prime parts. Also aperiodic partitions.
Narayana's cows sequence: a(0) = a(1) = a(2) = 1; thereafter a(n) = a(n-1) + a(n-3).
"Half-Catalan numbers": a(n) = Sum_{k=1..floor(n/2)} a(k)*a(n-k) with a(1) = 1.
Number of linear geometries on n (unlabeled) points.
Number of stacks, or arrangements of n pennies in contiguous rows, each touching 2 in row below.
Number of self-dual codes of length 2n over GF(4).
Number of filaments with n square cells.
Number of trees of diameter 4.
a(n) = number of compositions of n in which the maximum part size is 4.
Number of n-node rooted trees of height 4.
Binomial coefficient binomial(n,4) = n*(n-1)*(n-2)*(n-3)/24.
Number of permutations of [n] in which the longest increasing run has length 5.

Number of equivalence classes of n X n matrices over {0,1} with rows and columns summing to 5, where equivalence is defined by row and column permutations. Isomorphism classes of bicolored 5-regular bipartite graphs, where isomorphism cannot exchange the colors.
Number of tertiary alcohols (alkanols or alkyl alcohols C_n H_{2n+1} OH) with n carbon atoms.
Genus of complete graph on n nodes.
Number of permutations of length n with 4 consecutive ascending pairs.
One-half the number of permutations of length n with exactly 3 rising or falling successions.
a(n) is the number of partitions of n (the partition numbers).
Number of n-step spiral self-avoiding walks on hexagonal lattice, where at each step one may continue in same direction or make turn of 2*Pi/3 counterclockwise.
Number of partitions of n into at most 4 parts.
Number of partitions of n into at most 5 parts.
Number of partitions of n into at most 6 parts.
Smallest primitive prime factor of Fibonacci number F(n), or 1 if F(n) has no primitive prime factor.
a(n) = floor(3^n / 2^n).
Number of integers with a shortest addition chain of length n.
Number of rooted trees with n vertices in which vertices at the same level have the same degree.
a(0)=a(1)=1; thereafter a(n+1) = (1/n)*Sum_{k=0..n} a(k)^2 (a(n) is not always integral!).
Expansion of Product_{m >= 1} (1 + x^m); number of partitions of n into distinct parts; number of partitions of n into odd parts.
a(n) is the number of distinct (infinite) output sequences from binary n-stage shift register which feeds back the complement of the last stage.
Number of types of Latin squares of order n. Equivalently, number of nonisomorphic 1-factorizations of K_{n,n}.
Class number of Q(sqrt(-n)), n squarefree.
Dimension of the n-th graded piece of the mod-2 Steenrod algebra A_2.
Triangle of values of 2-d recurrence.
Number of symmetric filaments (strip polyominoes) with n square cells.
Numbers y such that p = x^2 + 7y^2, with prime p = A033207(n).
Number of nonisomorphic solutions to minimal independent dominating set on queens' graph Q(n).
Rotatable partitions.
Number of necklaces with n beads of 2 colors, allowing turning over (these are also called bracelets).

Number of n-bead necklaces with 2 colors when turning over is not allowed; also number of output sequences from a simple n-stage cycling shift register; also number of binary irreducible polynomials whose degree divides n.
Maximal number of pairwise relatively prime polynomials of degree n over GF(2).
Partial sums of A006206.
a(1) = 1; thereafter a(n+1) = floor(sqrt(2*a(n)*(a(n)+1))).
A Beatty sequence: floor(n * (sqrt(5) - 1)).
Highly abundant numbers: numbers k such that sigma(k) > sigma(m) for all m < k.
Number of divisors of n-th highly composite number.
Degree of rational Poncelet porism of n-gon.
Landau's function g(n): largest order of permutation of n elements. Equivalently, largest LCM of partitions of n.
a(n) is the number of partitions of n into at most 3 parts; also partitions of n+3 in which the greatest part is 3; also number of unlabeled multigraphs with 3 nodes and n edges.
Number of partitions with no even part repeated; partitions of n in which no parts are multiples of 4.
Number of point-symmetric tournaments with 2n+1 nodes.
Number of partitions of n into Fibonacci parts (with a single type of 1).
a(n) = ceiling((1 + sum of preceding terms) / 2) starting with a(0) = 1.
The limiting sequence [A259095(r(r+1)/2-s,r), s=0,1,2,...,r-1] for very large r.
Number of mixed Husimi trees with n nodes; or polygonal cacti with bridges.
Number of outcomes of unlabeled n-team round-robin tournaments.
Number of different score sequences that are possible in an n-team round-robin tournament.
Number of rooted ternary trees with n nodes; number of n-carbon alkyl radicals C(n)H(2n+1) ignoring stereoisomers.
Number of inequivalent planar partitions of n, when considering them as 3D objects.
Number of inequivalent n-gons.
Product of totient function: a(n) = Product_{k=1..n} phi(k) (cf. A000010).
Number of n-node rooted trees of height at most 3.
Number of n-node trees of height at most 4.
Number of n-node trees of height at most 5.
Number of representations of n as a sum of distinct Fibonacci numbers.
(1) Number of solutions to x^2 == 0 (mod n). (2) Also square root of largest square dividing n. (3) Also max_{ d divides n } gcd(d, n/d).
Number of solutions to x^3 == 0 (mod n).
Number of solutions to x^4 == 0 (mod n).
Number of Abelian groups of order n; number of factorizations of n into prime powers.
The multiplicative partition function: number of ways of factoring n with all factors greater than 1 (a(1) = 1 by convention).
Number of perfect partitions of n.
Positive y such that p = (x^2 + 27*y^2)/4 where p is the n-th prime of the form 6*k+1.
Number of ways of writing n as an unordered sum of at most 3 nonzero triangular numbers.
Fibonacci numbers: F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2) with F(0) = 0 and F(1) = 1.
a(n) = a(n-1) + a(n-2)^2 for n >= 2 with a(0) = 0 and a(1) = 1.
a(1)=0; for n>1, a(n) = number of isomeric hydrocarbons of the acetylene series with carbon content n.
Iccanobif numbers: reverse digits of two previous terms and add.
a(n) = Fibonacci(n-1) mod n.
a(n) = Fibonacci(n) mod 10.
Sum of digits of Fibonacci numbers.
Fibonacci numbers written backwards.
Reversals of Fibonacci numbers (sorted).
The characteristic function of {0}: a(n) = 0^n.
Number of asymmetric trees with n nodes (also called identity trees).
Number of labeled ordered set partitions into 5 parts for n>=1, a(0)=1.
Number of (unordered) ways of making change for n cents using coins of 5, 10, 20, 50, 100 cents.
Weight distribution of [ 23,12,7 ] binary perfect Golay code.
Nearest integer to Bernoulli number B_{2n}.
Low-temperature series for partition function for spin-1/2 Ising model on f.c.c. lattice.
Magnetization series for face-centered cubic lattice.
Number of asymmetric (not necessarily connected) graphs with n nodes.
Number of unlabeled identity interval graphs with n nodes.
Euler totient function phi(n): count numbers <= n and prime to n.
Number of n-bead necklaces (turning over is allowed) where complements are equivalent.
Definition (1): Number of n-bead binary necklaces with beads of 2 colors where the colors may be swapped but turning over is not allowed.
Number of positive integers <= 2^n of form x^2 + 16*y^2.
Number of partitions of n in which no parts are multiples of 3.
Number of ways of making change for n cents using coins of 1, 2, 4, 10, 20, 40, 100 cents.
Number of ways of making change for n cents using coins of 1, 2, 4, 10 cents.
Number of ways of making change for n cents using coins of 1, 2, 4, 12, 24, 48, 96, 120 cents (based on English coinage of 1939).

Reduced totient function psi(n): least k such that x^k == 1 (mod n) for all x prime to n; also known as the Carmichael lambda function (exponent of unit group mod n); also called the universal exponent of n.
Continued fraction for cube root of 5.
Powers of 2: a(n) = 2^n.
Maximal number of regions obtained by joining n points around a circle by straight lines. Also number of regions in 4-space formed by n-1 hyperplanes.
A nonlinear binomial sum.
Final two digits of 2^n.
Trajectory of 1 under map x->x + (x-with-digits-reversed).
A self-generating sequence: every positive integer occurs as a(i)-a(j) for a unique pair i,j.
Number of weighted linear spaces of total weight n.
RATS: Reverse Add Then Sort the digits applied to previous term, starting with 1.
Divisible only by primes congruent to 2 mod 7.
Factor complexity (number of subwords of length n) of the Golay-Rudin-Shapiro binary word A020987.
Tribonacci numbers: a(n) = a(n-1) + a(n-2) + a(n-3) for n >= 3 with a(0) = a(1) = 0 and a(2) = 1.
Number of n-node triangulations of sphere in which every node has degree >= 4.
Number of equivalence classes of n X n matrices over {0,1} with rows and columns summing to 3, where equivalence is defined by row and column permutations.
One half of number of non-self-conjugate partitions; also half of number of asymmetric Ferrers graphs with n nodes.
Number of cubic bicolored graphs on n unlabeled nodes admitting an automorphism exchanging the colors.
Number of free planar polyenoids with n nodes and symmetry point group C_{2v}.
Number of graphs with n nodes and n-3 edges.
The coding-theoretic function A(n,4,3).
Nearest integer to n^2/8.
From Goldbach conjecture: number of decompositions of 2n into an unordered sum of two odd primes.
Number of equivalence classes of nonzero regular 0-1 matrices of order n.
Numbers whose digits contain no loops (version 2).
Numbers k such that 4*k^2 + 1 is prime.
Numbers k such that 39*2^k + 1 is prime.
Numbers m such that 6m-1, 6m+1 are twin primes.
Numbers k such that 2*10^k - 1 is prime.
a(n) = smallest number with shortest addition chain of length n.
Cyclic numbers: k such that k and phi(k) are relatively prime; also k such that there is just one group of order k, i.e., A000001(k) = 1.
Ludic numbers: apply the same sieve as Eratosthenes, but cross off every k-th remaining number.
Expansion of (1+x)(1+x^2)/(1-x-x^3).
Tetranacci numbers: a(n) = a(n-1) + a(n-2) + a(n-3) + a(n-4) for n >= 4 with a(0) = a(1) = a(2) = 0 and a(3) = 1.

Number of equivalence classes of n X n matrices over {0,1} with rows and columns summing to 4, where equivalence is defined by row and column permutations. Also number of isomorphism classes of bicolored quartic bipartite graphs, where isomorphism cannot exchange the colors.
Nearest integer to log_10(n).
Number of monosubstituted alkanes C(n)H(2n+1)-X of the form shown in the Comments lines that are not stereoisomers.
Number of numbers == 0 (mod 3) in range 2^n to 2^(n+1) with odd number of 1's in binary expansion.
Number of quartic bicolored graphs on n unlabeled nodes admitting an automorphism exchanging the colors.
5-adic valuation of binomial(2*n,n): largest k such that 5^k divides binomial(2*n, n).
Number of graphs with n nodes and n-4 edges.
Nonnegative integers repeated 3 times.
Number of maximal collections of pairwise disjoint subsets {X,Y,Z} of {1, 2, ..., n} with X + Y = Z (as in A002849), with the property that n is in one of the subsets.
Initial digit of n.
Numbers of form x^2 + y^2 + 2*z^2.
Label a 1-cm ruler with digits 1 cm wide.
The nonnegative integers.
Numbers with an odd number of digits.
Palindromes in base 10.
Numbers k such that k^2 - k + 41 is prime.
Smallest multiple of n whose digits sum to n.
Decimal Gray code for n.
Read n backwards (referred to as R(n) in many sequences).
The positive integers. Also called the natural numbers, the whole numbers or the counting numbers, but these terms are ambiguous.
Euler's "numerus idoneus" (or "numeri idonei", or idoneal, or suitable, or convenient numbers).
Least number of 4th powers needed to represent n.
7-smooth numbers: positive numbers whose prime divisors are all <= 7.
Numbers that are divisible by each nonzero digit.
Fully multiplicative with a(prime(k)) = partition(k+1).
Numbers that are not the sum of 4 distinct squares.
Numbers that are not the sum of 5 distinct squares.
Numbers that are not the sum of 3 distinct nonzero squares.
Numbers that are not the sum of 4 distinct nonzero squares.
The zero sequence.
Genus of modular group Gamma_0(n). Or, genus of modular curve X_0(n).
Number of simple imperfect squared squares of order n up to symmetry.
Decimal expansion of Bohr radius (meters).
Number of connected trivalent graphs with 2n nodes and girth exactly 7.
Number of simple perfect squared squares of order n up to symmetry.
Non-Hamiltonian simplicial polyhedra with n nodes.
Non-Hamiltonian 1-tough simplicial polyhedra with n nodes.
Number of non-Hamiltonian polyhedra with n nodes.
Number of strict 5th-order maximal independent sets in cycle graph.
a(n) is the number of partitions of n into at most 3 parts; also partitions of n+3 in which the greatest part is 3; also number of unlabeled multigraphs with 3 nodes and n edges.
a(n) is the number of partitions of n (the partition numbers).
Molien series for A_9.
Molien series for A_10.
Molien series for A_11.
Molien series for alternating group Alt_12 (or A_12).
Number of partitions of n into at most 9 parts.
Number of partitions of n into at most 10 parts.
Number of partitions of n into at most 11 parts.
Number of partitions of n into at most 12 parts.
The simplest sequence of positive numbers: the all 1's sequence.
Class numbers associated with terms of A001986.
Class number of real quadratic field with discriminant 4n+1.
Class number of real quadratic field with discriminant A003658(n), n >= 2.
Number of laminated lattices of dimension n.
Triangle read by rows: T(n,k) is the number of simple regular bipartite graphs with 2n nodes and degree k, (0 <= k <= n).
Largest 4th power dividing n.
Number of ways of making change for n cents using coins of 1, 2, 5, 10 cents.
Denumerants: Expansion of 1/((1-x)*(1-x^2)*(1-x^5)).
Number of ways of making change for n cents using coins of 1, 2, 5, 10, 25 cents.
Number of ways of making change for n cents using coins of 1, 2, 5, 10, 25, 50 cents.
Number of ways of making change for n cents using coins of 1, 2, 5, 10, 50, 100 cents.
Number of ways of making change for n cents using coins of 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50 cents.

Numbers with no 3 adjacent 1's in binary expansion.
Delete all 7's from the sequence of nonnegative integers.
Numbers whose binary expansion does not begin 111.
Numbers not congruent to 7 mod 8.
Numbers that are the sum of at most 6 positive cubes.
A Beatty sequence: floor(n * (sqrt(5) - 1)).
Nonhypotenuse numbers (indices of positive squares that are not the sums of 2 distinct nonzero squares).
Numbers that are not the sum of 3 distinct squares.
Numbers that are not the sum of 2 distinct nonzero squares.
Smallest number that is not the sum of squares of two distinct earlier terms.

The coding-theoretic function A(n,14,9).
The coding-theoretic function A(n,14,10).

Clock chimes with a quarter-hour bell.
Triangle of partition numbers: T(n,k) = number of partitions of n in which the greatest part is k, 1 <= k <= n. Also number of partitions of n into k positive parts, 1 <= k <= n.
Triangle read by rows: T(n,k) is the number of simple regular connected bipartite graphs with 2n nodes and degree k, (2 <= k <= n).
Table T(n,k), n>=0 and k>=0, read by antidiagonals: the k-th column given by the k-th Narayana polynomial.

a(n) = floor(n/9)*ceiling(n/9).
a(n) = Product_{i=0..8} floor((n+i)/9).
Expansion of Product_{m >= 1} (1 + x^m); number of partitions of n into distinct parts; number of partitions of n into odd parts.
